explanation: RC5 is a block cipher with a variable block size (32, 64, or 128 bits), variable key size, and variable rounds; the others use fixed block sizes.

answerText: A. systems on a network that are sharing files, folders, and printers
explanation: Network share enumeration identifies hosts that expose shared resources (files, folders, printers) over the network.

answerText: A. when user credentials were not provided
explanation: An unauthenticated scan is run externally without login credentials, typically because none were provided or the goal is to simulate an outside attacker.
A Network Intrusion Detection System passively monitors traffic and alerts an administrator, but does not itself block traffic (that is the role of a NIPS).
Stateful inspection tracks the state of active connections/conversations to determine which return traffic should be permitted.
A three-leg (triple-homed) DMZ uses a single firewall with three interfaces: internal network, DMZ, and external/Internet.

answerText: B. Both are deployed as sensors.; D. Both use signatures to detect malicious traffic.
explanation: IDS and IPS are both deployed as sensors on the network and both commonly rely on signature databases to identify malicious traffic; they differ mainly in that IPS can act inline to block traffic.

Environmental metrics include modified base/CIA requirements (c); Base metrics include exploitability and impact metrics (b); Temporal metrics include exploit code maturity, remediation level, and report confidence (a).
The CVSS Environmental group includes modified base metrics and the confidentiality/integrity/availability requirements; the other items belong to Base or Temporal groups.

Process-level remediation, patch management, and secrets management are operational controls (day-to-day procedures carried out by people), as opposed to technical (system-enforced), administrative (policy-level), or physical controls.
CVSS Temporal metrics consist of exploit code maturity, remediation level, and report confidence, reflecting factors that change over time.
A brute-force attack systematically tries every possible key until the correct one decrypts the message.
Union-based SQL injection appends a UNION SELECT statement to force the database to return results from additional SELECT queries combined with the original query.

answerText: C. Use HTML escape before inserting untrusted data into HTML element content.; E. Use attribute escape before inserting untrusted data into HTML common attributes.; F. Use JavaScript escape before inserting untrusted data into JavaScript data values.
explanation: OWASP's XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet rules call for context-appropriate escaping (HTML escape, attribute escape, JavaScript escape) of untrusted data before inserting it into those contexts.

answerText: A. caching of HTTP messages; D. enabling HTTP transfers across a firewall
explanation: Web proxies primarily cache HTTP content to speed up access and act as intermediaries that allow HTTP traffic to traverse firewalls safely.
In the URL, xyz-company.com is the host (a), 2457 is the port (c), support/file is the path (d), and name=intro&r=true is the query-string (b).

answerText: B. These session ID names can be used to fingerprint the application framework employed.
explanation: Default session ID names (e.g., JSESSIONID, PHPSESSID) reveal which framework is running, helping attackers fingerprint the application and target known vulnerabilities.

answerText: B. to convince a victim on a phone call to disclose private or financial information
explanation: Vishing (voice phishing) uses phone calls to trick victims into revealing sensitive information.

Asterisk is an open-source VoIP/PBX management platform that can be configured to manipulate and spoof caller ID information, unlike SpoofCard/SpoofApp which are simple caller-ID spoofing apps rather than VoIP management tools.
Creating a false sense of urgency (act now or suffer consequences) is a classic influence technique used to pressure victims into clicking malicious links.

answerText: D. Shoulder surfing can be prevented by using special screen filters for computer displays.
explanation: Privacy screen filters restrict the viewing angle of a display, mitigating shoulder surfing. Statement A actually describes piggybacking (with consent) mislabeled as tailgating, and statement B describes tailgating (without consent) mislabeled as piggybacking, so both are reversed and incorrect; C is false since badge cloning can be performed with software-defined radio/RFID tools.

A partially known (gray-box) environment test provides the tester with limited information, such as domain names and IP addresses, rather than full internal knowledge (known/white-box) or none at all (unknown/black-box).

answerText: B. The tester is provided with a list of domain names and IP addresses in the scope of a particular target.
explanation: A partially known environment test is characterized by giving the tester limited scope information (domains/IPs) rather than full details or none at all.

answerText: A. Vulnerability Analysis; E. Trust Analysis
explanation: Vulnerability Analysis and Trust Analysis are core OSSTMM (Open Source Security Testing Methodology Manual) test channels and analytical processes, whereas network mapping and maintaining access are general pentesting activities.

answerText: A. Threat modeling; C. Reporting; F. Exploitation
explanation: PTES defines the phases: Pre-engagement, Intelligence Gathering, Threat Modeling, Vulnerability Analysis, Exploitation, Post-Exploitation, and Reporting; Threat modeling, Exploitation, and Reporting are named PTES phases, unlike Penetration, Enumerating further, or Network mapping.

answerText: A. use reputable and standard libraries to create the APIs; B. make internal API documentation mandatory
explanation: Using reputable, standard libraries to build APIs reduces security flaws, and maintaining thorough internal API documentation ensures developers and security auditors understand API design and boundaries.
Boolean operators and conditionals are logic constructs that control program flow; arrays, dictionaries, and libraries are data structures/resources, not logic constructs.

Zenmap (Nmap GUI) and Enum4linux both actively interact with and probe the target system directly, which characterizes active reconnaissance, unlike passive/OSINT tools such as Nslookup queries, Tor, and Maltego.
BlackArch Linux is an Arch-based penetration testing distribution whose repository has historically included over 1900 (now 2500+) security tools, more than Kali or Parrot ship by default.
Needle is an open-source framework specifically built for security testing of iOS applications; Drozer, APK Studio, and ApkX target Android.

answerText: A. turbines in a power plant; B. robots in a factory
explanation: Industrial control/OT systems like power plant turbines and factory robots control critical physical processes and should never be directly Internet-exposed, unlike typical consumer IoT devices such as thermostats, refrigerators, or CO detectors.
Credential harvesting involves breaching cloud infrastructure to collect valid usernames, passwords, tokens, and PINs for later misuse.

Meterpreter supports the standard Unix-style commands pwd (print working directory) and cat (display file contents), which also work natively on Linux/Unix systems; hashdump, clearev, and resource are Meterpreter-specific.
Account takeover occurs when a threat actor gains access to a user or application account, which can then be leveraged to access further accounts and information.

answerText: A. using insecure permission configurations for cloud object storage services; B. using hard-coded credentials to access different services
explanation: Misconfigured, overly permissive storage buckets and hard-coded credentials embedded in code/scripts are two leading causes of cloud data breaches.

answerText: A. testing timeline; C. location of testing; E. preferred method of communication
explanation: A Rules of Engagement (RoE) document explicitly details the testing timeline, physical or virtual testing locations, and preferred communication methods during a penetration test engagement.
PGP and S/MIME are the standard protocols for encrypting and digitally signing email to ensure secure, authenticated communication.
GraphQL is a query language for APIs along with a runtime for executing those queries against your data, matching the description exactly.
Under HIPAA, a healthcare clearinghouse converts nonstandard health information received from another entity into a standard format (or vice versa).
An amplification attack abuses services that return responses much larger than the request, magnifying the reflected traffic sent to the victim in a DDoS attack.

answerText: A. limit anonymous logins to a select group of people; E. require re-authentication of inactive sessions
explanation: Restricting anonymous FTP access and forcing re-authentication after inactivity reduce the attack surface and abuse of FTP servers; the other options are unrelated or counterproductive.

answerText: C. It is a post-exploitation attempt that is used to extract service account credential hashes from Active Directory for offline cracking.
explanation: Kerberoasting requests service tickets (TGS) for service accounts and extracts their encrypted hashes for offline brute-force/cracking to recover the service account password.
Wi-Fi Protected Setup (WPS) uses an 8-digit PIN with a design flaw that makes it highly vulnerable to brute-force attacks, unlike WPA2-EAP, WPA3, or even WPA2-TKIP.
Netcat is a lightweight, portable networking utility widely used to create bind and reverse shells from a compromised host.
Twittor and Empire are command-and-control (C2) frameworks/utilities used to manage compromised hosts, whereas Netcat, BloodHound, and Socket serve other purposes.
In a reverse shell, the attacker sets up a listener and the compromised victim system initiates the outbound connection back to the attacker, often to bypass inbound firewall restrictions.

answerText: A. that the port is open
explanation: In a TCP FIN scan, closed ports respond with an RST packet, while open (or open|filtered) ports simply ignore the FIN packet and send no response.

answerText: A. Vulnerability of services running inside the target may not be detected.
explanation: Without valid credentials, an unauthenticated scan can only assess externally visible attack surface and may miss vulnerabilities in services/software running locally on the target.
A Certificate Revocation List (CRL) is the published list of certificates that have been revoked/invalidated before their expiration date.
